sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) the inmost recess, adytum, of Solomon’s temple, elsewhere called קֹדֶשׁ קֳדָשִׁים 1Ki 6:5, -1922 ; 8:6 , 1Ki 8:8 2Ch 3:16 4:20 5:7 , 2Ch 5:9. Jerome translates it “oraculum, oraculi sedes” (from דִּבֶּר to speak [a far better rendering than the one proposed by Gesenius]); but it can hardly be doubted but that it properly is the hinder part, i.e. the western (see אָחֹור No. 2 ), as has been rightly observed by Iken, in Dissert. Philol. Theol. part i. p. 214.

(2) [Debir], pr.n.-(a) of a town in the tribe of Judah, elsewhere called Kirjath Sepher, Jud 1:11.-[“(b) of a town in the south of the tribe of Gad.-(c) of a king of Eglon.”]
